data.title: China Next-Gen Commerce Swings to Interim Loss on Revenue Slide
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

An update from S&T Holdings Ltd. ( (HK:3928) ) is now available.

China Next-Gen Commerce and Supply Chain Limited reported a sharp deterioration in performance for the six months ended 31 March 2026, as total revenue fell to S$16.3 million from S$32.0 million a year earlier, driven mainly by a halving of services income. Gross profit contracted to S$0.8 million, while higher administrative and selling expenses, increased credit loss allowances and finance costs pushed the group to a net loss of S$5.6 million, compared with a profit of S$0.4 million in the prior-year period, signaling mounting pressure on margins and operational efficiency for shareholders and creditors.

More about S&T Holdings Ltd.

China Next-Gen Commerce and Supply Chain Limited is a Cayman Islands–incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating in the commerce and supply chain services sector. The group generates revenue from service fees, rental income and sales of goods and services, focusing on integrated commercial and logistics solutions.

data.title: Starcoin Extends Bond Amendment Deadline and Delays Shareholder Circular Amid Trading Halt
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech ( (HK:0399) ) has provided an announcement.

Starcoin Group Limited has agreed with bondholder Extrawell Pharmaceutical Holdings Limited to extend the long stop date for fulfilling conditions attached to a fourth amendment of its convertible bonds, moving the deadline from 30 June 2026 to 30 September 2026. All other terms of the amendment deed remain unchanged, preserving the existing structure of the bond arrangement.

The company has also postponed the dispatch of a shareholder circular detailing the bond amendments and a related special general meeting, pushing the latest expected mailing date to 11 September 2026. Trading in Starcoin shares, suspended since 6 May 2026, will continue to be halted until further notice, leaving shareholders and potential investors facing ongoing uncertainty around liquidity and the timing of corporate approvals.

More about Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech

Starcoin Group Limited, formerly known as Innovative Pharmaceutical Biotech Limited, is a Hong Kong-listed company incorporated in the Cayman Islands and continued in Bermuda with limited liability. The group has issued convertible bonds to Extrawell Pharmaceutical Holdings Limited and remains subject to Hong Kong Stock Exchange listing rules and trading suspension procedures.

data.title: Sigenergy Revamps Governance and Chinese Name as It Plans 2025 Final Dividend
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Sigenergy Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:6656) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Sigenergy Technology plans to abolish its supervisory committee and transfer its oversight functions to the board’s audit committee, aligning its corporate governance with recent revisions to China’s Company Law and updated CSRC guidelines for listed companies. The move is intended to modernize its governance framework without disrupting daily operations or shareholder rights.

The company also proposes changing its Chinese name, while keeping the English name and stock short name unchanged, to better reflect its actual business, global strategy, and market positioning. These changes, which require shareholder and regulatory approvals, aim to strengthen Sigenergy’s market influence and sharpen investor perception of its value as it continues to expand internationally.

In addition, the board announced a plan to distribute a final dividend for the year ended December 31, 2025, signaling confidence in the company’s financial performance and offering a direct benefit to shareholders.

More about Sigenergy Technology Co., Ltd. Class H

Sigenergy Technology Co., Ltd. is a Shanghai-registered company positioned as a leading global provider of stackable distributed optical storage all-in-one energy systems. Its products are exported to nearly 100 countries and regions, reflecting a strong focus on international markets and a strategy to align its branding with its global development and main business structure.

data.title: Black Sesame International moves to modernise governance with new electronic-friendly constitution
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

Black Sesame International Holding Limited ( (HK:2533) ) just unveiled an update.

Black Sesame International Holding Limited, a Cayman Islands-incorporated issuer listed in Hong Kong, is overseen by a board that includes executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, in line with standard corporate governance practices for companies on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong. The company’s legal and governance arrangements are anchored in the Hong Kong Listing Rules and Cayman Islands law, which shape how it manages shareholder rights, disclosure and meeting procedures.

The board plans to amend its existing memorandum and articles of association to align with the expanded paperless listing regime, enabling hybrid general meetings, electronic voting and electronic submission of shareholder instructions such as proxy appointments. These proposed changes, which also include related housekeeping updates to comply with Hong Kong Listing Rules and Cayman Islands requirements, will be bundled into a new set of constitutional documents subject to shareholder approval by special resolution at the upcoming annual general meeting.

A circular detailing the proposed amendments and the adoption of the new memorandum and articles of association, along with the AGM notice, will be published on the company and exchange websites in due course. The initiative underscores the company’s effort to modernise its shareholder engagement processes and strengthen regulatory compliance by embracing electronic communication and meeting formats in line with broader market trends.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:2533) stock is a Sell with a HK$16.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Black Sesame International Holding Limited stock, see the HK:2533 Stock Forecast page.

More about Black Sesame International Holding Limited

Black Sesame International Holding Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange under stock code 2533. It is governed by a board comprising executive, non-executive and independent non-executive directors, reflecting a typical Hong Kong-listed corporate governance structure focused on compliance with local market regulations.

The company operates within the Hong Kong capital markets framework and is subject to the Listing Rules and Cayman Islands law. Its governance and shareholder communication mechanisms are aligned with evolving exchange requirements, including the move toward paperless processes and greater use of electronic and hybrid meeting formats for listed issuers.

Gala Technology Holding Limited ( (HK:2458) ) has shared an announcement.

Gala Technology Holding Limited has called its annual general meeting for 25 June 2026 in Shenzhen, where shareholders will vote on adopting the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and reappointing Grant Thornton Hong Kong as independent auditor. The meeting will also consider the re-election of two independent non-executive directors and a mandate allowing the board to issue up to 20% of the company’s share capital, a step that could provide additional financial flexibility and support future capital-raising or strategic initiatives.

These resolutions, if approved, would reaffirm the company’s existing governance arrangements while equipping directors with broader authority over equity issuance within prescribed limits. The share issuance mandate, excluding treasury shares and specific exempted scenarios such as rights issues and share option schemes, may influence future dilution dynamics and capital structure decisions, and signals management’s intent to retain optionality for funding growth or other corporate actions.

By renewing the auditor’s appointment and empowering the board on director remuneration, Gala Technology underscores continuity in its oversight and reporting framework. The AGM outcomes will be closely watched by investors for any indication of shifts in corporate strategy, governance priorities, or balance sheet management that could affect shareholder value.

More about Gala Technology Holding Limited

Gala Technology Holding Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed in Hong Kong, operating within the technology sector and subject to the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company maintains an independent board structure with non-executive directors and engages external auditors to review its consolidated financial statements for shareholders.

Black Sesame International Holding Limited has called an extraordinary general meeting for June 17, 2026, to seek shareholder approval on matters related to a previously announced acquisition. The company will issue a circular in due course setting out the proposed resolution and formal notice of the meeting on both the Hong Kong Stock Exchange and its own website. data.title: Cirtek Moves to Replace PwC Amid Audit Dispute and Results Delay
data.description:
Memorial Day Sale – Claim 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock trusted, data-backed investing tools with TipRanks Premium, from analyst ratings and forecasts to breaking news and portfolio analysis.
  • Discover high-conviction stock picks and new investing opportunities with the TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter

The latest announcement is out from Cirtek Holdings Limited ( (HK:1433) ).

Cirtek Holdings plans to remove PricewaterhouseCoopers as its auditor and appoint Grant Thornton Hong Kong, subject to shareholder approval at an extraordinary general meeting, after a dispute over the handling of an investigation into certain payments by an overseas subsidiary. The disagreement, centered on audit arrangements and the composition of the Bangladesh component audit team, has contributed to a delay in publishing the 2025 annual results and a continued suspension of trading in the company’s shares, underscoring governance and transparency concerns for investors and regulators.

PwC had raised red flags over payments recorded under property, plant and equipment and other expenses, recommending an independent investigation by reputable professional investigators and legal counsel. The firm stated it had not received sufficient information about the proposed investigators, legal advisers, or the scope of the probe to assess their competence and independence, and it maintained that the existing component audit team, which identified the issues, should remain in place, highlighting a serious breakdown in alignment between the auditor and the company’s management and audit committee.

More about Cirtek Holdings Limited

Cirtek Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated investment holding company listed in Hong Kong, with operations that include overseas subsidiaries such as in Bangladesh. The group’s activities involve owning and managing property, plant and equipment and incurring related operating expenses across its international footprint, exposing it to multi-jurisdictional audit and compliance requirements.

CTR Holdings Limited ( (HK:1416) ) has issued an update.

CTR Holdings Limited has updated the terms of reference for its Nomination Committee, originally established in 2019, to ensure continued compliance with the Hong Kong Stock Exchange’s Listing Rules and to formalize its governance framework around board appointments. The revised terms specify that the committee must comprise at least three directors with a majority being independent non-executive directors, include at least one member of a different gender, and clarify procedures for appointments, removals, meeting notices, and tenure, underscoring the company’s focus on board diversity, transparency, and regulatory alignment.

The updated mandate also sets out clear rules for convening meetings, including notice periods and acceptable communication methods, which is expected to streamline committee operations and enhance accountability in nomination processes. These changes reinforce CTR Holdings’ corporate governance structure, potentially improving investor confidence by demonstrating a structured approach to board oversight and diversity as regulatory expectations in Hong Kong continue to evolve.

More about CTR Holdings Limited

CTR Holdings Limited is a Cayman Islands-incorporated company listed on the Hong Kong Stock Exchange, operating under the governance framework required for Hong Kong-listed issuers. Its board structure includes committees such as the Nomination Committee, which oversees director appointments and board composition to align with regulatory standards and corporate governance best practices.

Ever Reach Group Holdings Co., Ltd. ( (HK:3616) ) has provided an update.

Ever Reach Group has agreed to sell its entire 70% equity stake in Xuchang Yuanda Real Estate Company Limited, an indirect non-wholly owned subsidiary, to Xuchang Fengzhida Real Estate Company Limited for RMB3 million. The disposal means Ever Reach will cease to hold any interest in the target, which will no longer be consolidated into the group’s financial statements, slightly reshaping its asset base and project exposure.

The transaction is classified as both a discloseable and connected transaction under Hong Kong listing rules because the purchaser is a substantial shareholder of the target and therefore a connected person at the subsidiary level. With board approval and confirmation by independent non-executive directors that the terms are fair, reasonable and on normal commercial terms, the deal requires only public announcement, streamlining execution while signaling governance safeguards for minority shareholders.

More about Ever Reach Group Holdings Co., Ltd.

Bank of Chongqing has called its 2025 annual general meeting for June 23, 2026 in Chongqing, where shareholders will review the 2025 board work report, final financial accounts and annual report, and vote on the 2025 profit distribution plan and the 2026 financial budget. Hong Kong Finance Group Ltd. ( (HK:1273) ) has provided an update.

Hong Kong Finance Group has agreed to extend a new secured loan of HK$4 million to three repeat customers under a nine-month facility carrying an interest rate of 18% per annum. The loan is backed by a second mortgage over two residential units and two car parking spaces on Conduit Road, Hong Kong, valued at about HK$148 million by an independent valuer.

Including this advance, the group has provided a total of HK$60 million in loans to these borrowers within a year, requiring aggregation under Hong Kong listing rules and triggering a discloseable transaction classification. The company says the short tenure, strong collateral coverage, and solid financial background and repayment history of key borrowers render the credit risk manageable for its lending portfolio.

More about Hong Kong Finance Group Ltd.

Hong Kong Finance Group Limited is a Hong Kong-based financial services company engaged in money lending activities through its subsidiary HK Finance. The group focuses on providing secured loans to customers, often backed by real estate collateral in prime locations, and operates under the regulatory framework of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange.

Midea Real Estate Holding Limited ( (HK:3990) ) has shared an update.

Midea Real Estate Holding Limited has called its annual general meeting for 24 June 2026, to be held both physically in Foshan, Guangdong, and online via a virtual platform. Shareholders will review and adopt the audited financial statements for the year ended 31 December 2025 and vote on a proposed final dividend of HK$0.19 per share.

The agenda also includes the re-election of Mr. Zhao Jun as a non-executive director and Mr. Lu Qi as an independent non-executive director, along with authorization for the board to set directors’ pay. Investors will further vote on re-appointing PricewaterhouseCoopers as auditor and on granting the board a general mandate to issue up to 20% of existing share capital, which could provide the company with flexibility for future capital-raising and strategic initiatives.
